qa_pairs.question: How do you execute an Ansys Rocky simulation in non-GUI (headless) mode using either a CPU-only or GPU-based Slurm script on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To run an Ansys Rocky simulation in non-GUI (headless) mode, you use the `Rocky --headless --simulate` command within your Slurm script. For a CPU-only simulation, include `--use-gpu=0` and specify the number of CPUs with `--ncpus=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK`. For a GPU-based simulation, include `--use-gpu=1` along with `--ncpus=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK`, and ensure your Slurm script requests GPU resources (e.g., `#SBATCH --gpus=h100:1`). In both cases, you must specify the input file, for example, `mySim.rocky`.

qa_pairs.question: In an Ansys Rocky Slurm script, how do you configure it to start a new simulation versus resuming an existing one?
qa_pairs.answer: You configure this using the `RESUME` variable in your Slurm script. If you set `RESUME=0`, the script will start a new simulation and automatically remove any previous results located in the `$INPUTFILE.files/simulation` directory. If you set `RESUME=1`, the script will resume the simulation from its last saved state without deleting prior data.
